title: Kaukenia commersonii
taxonomy: Kaukenia commersonii is classified within the family Polygalaceae. This family, also known as the milkwort family, comprises approximately 840 species across 85 genera. The genus Kaukenia is a monotypic genus, consisting of only one species, Kaukenia commersonii. Kaukenia commersonii is native to New Caledonia, an island country in the Pacific Ocean. Its classification is part of the broader plant taxonomy, which groups organisms based on shared characteristics and evolutionary relationships.
uses: Kaukenia commersonii is a plant native to various parts of the world, and its medicinal properties are being studied for potential health benefits. Traditionally, extracts from this plant have been used in some cultures to treat various ailments, including fever and inflammation. Research suggests that it may have antimicrobial and antiviral properties, which could be useful in combating certain infections. Further studies are needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human use. Potential applications of Kaukenia commersonii in modern medicine are still being explored.

preparations: Kaukenia commersonii. It is a plant native to New Caledonia, and its herbal preparations are primarily used in traditional medicine.
